methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Enapter operates in the Climate & Energy sector as a public-stage company. The comparable universe for valuation consists of 1 public peer (LanzaTech, ticker LNZA) and 8 private competitors, including QuantumScape, TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, and Helion. This represents a total of 9 identifiable comps across the broader climate and energy technology landscape.

The peer-median triangulation method is not applicable to this analysis. A narrow peer set screening—filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier companies—yielded fewer than the three datapoints required for statistical reliability. Specifically, the Climate & Energy sector contains 21 same-sector peers overall, but application of stage and tier constraints identified only 2 same-stage comparables and zero adjacent-stage or similar-tier matches. Reliance on an all-sector median would introduce material distortion and is therefore excluded from this valuation.

The limited comparable base and absence of sufficiently tight peer clustering constrains the applicability of market-based valuation approaches. Alternative methodologies such as discounted cash flow analysis or bottom-up revenue multiple frameworks would be necessary to triangulate a defensible valuation range for Enapter. Key uncertainties include the early-stage or pre-revenue status of most private peers, heterogeneity in underlying technology platforms across the climate tech stack, and scarcity of recent transaction data within narrowly defined subsectors.
